alerts.title: Iran rebuilding missile production and underground 'missile cities' faster than expected
alerts.summary: The Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post report Iran is reconstituting ballistic missile production faster than US-Israeli planners anticipated. Rybar (~87K views, citing leaked NYT-stage US intelligence and satellite imagery) corroborates that the IRGC has 'practically restored' the underground missile-city tunnel entrances damaged in earlier US/Israeli strikes — undercutting the premise of the 'largely negotiated' Hormuz framework while threat level remains 9/CRITICAL.

alerts.title: Larestan missile base reportedly repaired and back in operation near Persian Gulf
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator (~54K views) reports that the entrance to the Larestan underground missile complex in southern Iran near the Persian Gulf has been repaired and the base is now operational again. The reactivation, paired with broader IRGC reconstitution reporting, places renewed missile threat directly on US naval assets in the Gulf, including USS Florida (SSGN) tracked at ~26°N, 51°E.

alerts.title: Iran says Mojtaba Khamenei suffered only 'superficial' wounds in US-Israel strike
alerts.summary: Türkiye Today, The Straits Times and Breakingthenews.net report Iranian health officials confirming that Mojtaba Khamenei — son of and possible successor to the Supreme Leader — was wounded in a recent US-Israel strike, characterized by Tehran as 'superficial.' The acknowledgement is the first public Iranian confirmation that strikes reached the Supreme Leader's immediate family, a significant escalation threshold even as Tehran insists no deal is imminent.

alerts.title: Republican hawks publicly attack Trump's emerging Iran deal as 'nightmare for Israel'
alerts.summary: Middle East Eye and parallel NYT analysis report Republican hawks, joined by Rep. Massie (cited 6x in US sentiment word cloud), are openly attacking Trump's emerging Iran framework, with NYT framing Netanyahu as reduced from 'co-pilot' to 'mere passenger.' US sentiment remains 56% negative with 'trump,' 'iran,' and 'deal' dominating discourse, signaling domestic political constraints on any Hormuz-reopening agreement.

alerts.title: US deploying defense 'umbrella' of tech and assets to shield Strait of Hormuz shipping
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is rolling out an integrated 'umbrella' of defensive and technology assets to protect commercial shipping transiting the Strait of Hormuz, as GPS jamming continues to disrupt navigation across the Middle East (The Week). The posture aligns with USS Abraham Lincoln deployed in the Arabian Sea (~21°N, 60°E) and USS Florida in the Persian Gulf, indicating Washington is hedging the deal track with hardened force protection.

analysis_note: US-Iran negotiations are visibly fraying as Tehran rebuilds missile infrastructure, confirms a strike wounded the Supreme Leader's son, and Republican hawks revolt against Trump's framework — even as the US hardens shipping defenses around Hormuz.
